title: Compare life insurance policies
description: Practice understanding of group life, term life, and whole life insurance

assessment_items.question_content:
When shopping for life insurance, people often look at the cost.

**Arrange life insurance options from least to most expensive.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: The life insurance you get through your job usually covers a small amount, but it’s really cheap or even free sometimes.
Term life insurance usually costs less than whole life but only covers you for a specific period, like $10$, $20$, or $30$ years.
Whole life insurance tends to be most expensive because it covers you for your entire life and includes a savings component.

Life insurance options, from least to most expensive are:

$\boxed{\text{group life}}$ $\boxed{\text{term life}}$ $\boxed{\text{whole life}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
When shopping for life insurance, people often look at the cost.

**Arrange life insurance options from most to least expensive.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Whole life insurance tends to be most expensive because it covers you for your entire life and includes a savings component.
Term life insurance usually costs less than whole life but only covers you for a specific period, like $10$, $20$, or $30$ years.
The life insurance you get through your job usually covers a small amount, but it’s really cheap or even free sometimes.

Life insurance options, from most to least expensive are:

$\boxed{\text{whole life}}$ $\boxed{\text{term life}}$ $\boxed{\text{group life}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
When shopping for life insurance, people often look at the cost.

**Which life insurance option is the cheapest?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You're healthy now, but your family has a history of health problems. You want a life insurance policy that lasts a lifetime and stays active, even if your health declines and you become uninsurable in the future.

**Which policy works best for this purpose?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You’re dealing with some health issues and just starting out in the workforce. With few family responsibilities, you’re interested in life insurance but don't have much extra money to pay for a policy.

**Which policy works best for this purpose?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You just bought a house with a mortgage. You want to make sure your family can cover the payments if something happens to you.

**Which policy would work best for this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

While some people choose whole life insurance because of its lifetime benefit, there is one major drawback to it.

**What is a drawback of whole life insurance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It requires a full medical exam.
It ends when you leave your job.
It typically offers smaller coverage amounts.

assessment_items.question_content:
While some people choose group life insurance because of its free or low cost, there is one major drawback to it.

**What is a drawback of group life insurance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A full medical exam is required.
It ends when you leave the employer.
It expires after pre-determined time frame.

assessment_items.question_content:
While some people choose term life insurance because of the amount of high coverage available, there is one major drawback to it.

**What is a drawback of term life insurance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It costs a lot for coverage.
Once it expires you may not be able to renew it.
It has no expiration date, so you pay forever.

assessment_items.question_content:
As we go though life, our life insurance needs can change. 

**Which of these events might mean you need *more* coverage?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

As we go though life, our life insurance needs can change. 

**Which life event might indicate you need *more* coverage?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

As we go though life, our life insurance needs can change. 

**Which of these events might mean you need *less* coverage?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: your stay-at-home spouse gets a job
